title: Alert Deduplication
description: Implement alert deduplication to reduce alert noise. Given a log of alerts with timestamps, suppress duplicate alerts of the same type that occur within a sliding window of seconds from the last kept alert. Return only the non-suppressed alerts in their original order.
constraints: Keep the first occurrence of an alert; suppress subsequent occurrences within window_seconds
After window_seconds have passed since the last kept alert, the next occurrence is kept
Return kept alert lines joined with newlines

examples.explanation: HighCPU at 1000 is kept. HighCPU at 1100 is suppressed (1100-1000=100s < 300s window). HighMemory at 1400 is a new alert, kept. HighCPU at 1600 is kept (1600-1000=600s > 300s window).

starter_code.python:
def deduplicate_alerts(log_content: str, window_seconds: int = 300) -> str:
    pass

test_cases.description: HighCPU deduped within window, re-fires after window expires
Different alerts are never suppressed against each other
Three rapid same-name alerts, only first survives
